notes: Information Science students should move through the early INSS sequence before later systems planning.
Information Systems students should build business data communication context before upper Information Systems work.
Students should build enough lower-division context before advanced Information Systems planning.
Analytics and decision-support coursework should follow the lower Information Systems sequence.
Cloud Computing students should build programming literacy early.
Infrastructure and platform foundations should come before advanced cloud work.
Cloud Computing students benefit from systems-oriented programming before upper cloud work.
Mid-level cloud progression should precede platform design and security-heavy coursework.
Advanced cloud security should wait for lower progression.
Distributed cloud architecture should follow the broader cloud sequence.
Nursing students should stay on pace with lower-division science preparation.
Introduction to Nursing should stay early in the sequence.
Students should finish the intro biology progression before upper nursing work.
Foundations of Nursing Practice should precede later nursing progression.
Health Assessment should come before upper nursing coursework.
Upper nursing work should wait until the earlier nursing sequence is in place.
Community and population health nursing should follow the earlier nursing sequence.
Biology students should finish the intro sequence before upper biology.
Biology students should build core supporting science preparation early.
The chemistry sequence should stay ahead of upper biology planning.
Upper biology should follow the intro science sequence.
Advanced biology should wait for the earlier biology progression.
Psychology students should move beyond the intro course before advanced psych planning.
Statistics should come before later psychology planning.
Research Methods should stay ahead of advanced psychology topics.
Experimental Psychology should follow methods preparation.
